get.description:
Returns aggregated analytics data for the authenticated company.

This endpoint is typically used to power dashboard views in frontend applications.

Includes:
- Total number of jobs posted
- Number of active jobs
- Total number of shortlisted candidates across all jobs

Requires a valid Bearer token.

Authorization header format:
Authorization: Bearer <token>

Returns gap analysis BEFORE applying.

Does not persist data.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Job to preview gap analysis against

200.description: Gap preview with updated scorecard, skills gap, and review outcome.

data.gapSummary: Review-recommended candidate. High backend capability. Missing Rust experience.

post.description:
Allows an authenticated candidate to apply for an ACTIVE job.

This triggers:
- Gap analysis
- Decision card generation
- Initial pipeline stage = APPLIED

get.description: Returns candidate applications with human-readable pipeline stages.

Returns all applications for a job including:
- HR decision view
- Technical evaluation
- Candidate info

Supports filtering.

Returns full application data including:
- Decision card
- Gap report
- Candidate profiles
- Interview questions

200.description: Returns application detail with dual-view shape (HR vs CTO)

hrView.hrSummary: Strong candidate with clear career growth

technicalView.technicalSummary: Expert in React and Node.js. Missing Rust experience.

scorecard.summary: Full-stack developer with strong backend capability.

patch.description: Marks an application as SHORTLISTED, REJECTED, or REVIEWED.

get.description: Generates a printable HTML scorecard for external sharing.

patch.description:
Moves candidate forward in hiring pipeline.

Validates transitions and may generate interview questions.

get.description:
Returns generated interview questions.

Includes the frozen final scorecard and skills gap context used for technical probing.

Optional:
- Filter by stage
- Defaults to latest set

200.description: Interview question set with scorecard and skills gap context.

questions.question: How would you design a Rust service for this role?

post.description: Generates a mock scorecard for a given GitHub username and returns a frontend-ready UI model. Requires internal API key.

401.description: Missing or invalid internal API key

post.description: Returns full raw scoring data for debugging and internal analysis. Not intended for frontend usage.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user’s scorecard formatted for frontend display.

404.description: No scorecard found. User must trigger GitHub sync first.

get.description: Fetch a cached scorecard for a GitHub username. Returns frontend-ready UI model.

404.description: No cached scorecard found. Must trigger analysis first.

get.description: Returns raw internal scorecard data for the authenticated user.
Returns raw cached scorecard for debugging or internal usage.
Returns a fully populated AnalysisResult including deployedPrograms with upgradeCount and Superteam achievements. Use this to develop wallet-related UI.
Returns an AnalysisResult where web3 is null. Use to verify UI renders correctly without wallet data.
Returns an AnalysisResult with low-confidence GitHub signals and on-chain wallet data only.
Returns all three mock AnalysisResult variants in one response. Useful for component story setup.
Serves a standalone HTML page that renders all mock API responses for frontend developers.

post.description:
Creates or retrieves an analysis job for a developer.

Modes:
1. Authenticated: Reads linked GitHub/Wallet from profile.
2. Anonymous: Takes identifiers from body.

Features:
- Cache check (skip if force=true)
- GitHub snapshot reuse (<24h)
- Async execution via BullMQ

requestBody.description:
Optional if authenticated.

- If JWT is provided → body is ignored (uses linked accounts)
- If no JWT → must provide githubUsername or walletAddress

post.description:
Triggers a fresh analysis.

- If force=true → cache is invalidated
- Requires internal API key (Bearer token)

Use this for admin/system reprocessing.

401.description: Missing or invalid internal API key

get.description: Returns current job state, stage, and progress percentage.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: BullMQ job ID returned when creating analysis

get.description: Returns final analysis result if completed.
Generates a GitHub OAuth URL for the authenticated user and redirects them to GitHub authorization.

200.description: Redirects user to GitHub OAuth consent screen

get.description: Handles GitHub OAuth callback and links GitHub account to the user profile, then triggers initial sync.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: OAuth state parameter used for CSRF protection

200.description: Redirects user to frontend sync progress page

post.description: Manually triggers a sync of GitHub data for the authenticated user. Requires GitHub to be connected. This step is required before generating a scorecard for a signed in user

200.description: Sync job successfully queued or executed

409.description: GitHub account not connected. Frontend should redirect to connect flow.

get.description: Returns current sync state including progress, errors, and last sync timestamp.
Returns a list of published jobs with filtering and pagination.

get.description:
Returns all job posts owned by the authenticated company, ordered by newest first.

Useful for dashboards and job management views.
get.summary: Get all jobs created by the authenticated company

get.description: Returns detailed information about a published job.

post.description:
Creates a job in DRAFT status for the authenticated company.

Use this endpoint when a company wants to start drafting a job listing before publishing it.
post.summary: Create a new job post draft

post.description:
Uses AI to extract structured requirements (skills, seniority, weights).

Frontend should use this for preview before confirming requirements.
post.summary: Parse job description into structured requirements

post.description:
Persists AI-parsed requirements into the job.

This step finalizes the structured scoring configuration.

Changes job status from DRAFT to ACTIVE.

Once published, the job becomes visible to candidates.

404.description: Job not found or does not belong to user

post.description:
Marks the job as CLOSED.

Closed jobs are no longer visible or accepting applications.

Verifies a Privy access token from frontend authentication and returns a signed JWT for API access. 
TESTING:
 1. make sure .env > PRIVY_BYPASS="true"
2. Bearer Token = debugtoken 
3. Authorization header = did:privy:test-user-123

Invalidates refresh token and clears session cookies.

Resets the password with a valid password reset token.

404.description: Company not found for authenticated user

503.description: The Health Check is not successful

get.description: Returns authenticated user profile including linked auth providers (Google, GitHub, etc).

patch.description: Updates first name, last name, and username.

delete.description: Marks the user account as suspended.

get.description: Returns authoritative cooldown timestamps for the authenticated user.
Returns candidate-specific data including bio, career path, and dev profile.
Returns whether GitHub is connected and sync status (without exposing tokens).
Returns whether wallet is connected and sync status to which address.

post.description:
Creates and anchors a vouch for a candidate using a Solana transaction.
  
  This endpoint:
  - Verifies the transaction exists on-chain
  - Confirms the voucher wallet is the fee payer
  - Validates memo message matches the provided message
  - Enforces rules: no self-vouch, no duplicates, budget limits
  
  Authentication: JWT required. Uses the linked Solana wallet.

requestBody.description: Payload required to confirm a vouch

verifiedVoucher.summary: Successful vouch from a high-quality (verified) wallet

newWalletVoucher.summary: Vouch created from a new wallet (lower trust weight)

400.description: Validation error, duplicate vouch, self-vouch, budget exceeded, or invalid transaction

duplicate.summary: User already vouched for this candidate

selfVouch.summary: User attempted to vouch for themselves

value.message: No Memo instruction found whose text matches the provided message
Candidate not found for identifier: octocat

delete.description:
Revokes an existing vouch.
  
  Authentication: JWT required. Uses the linked Solana wallet.

requestBody.description: Wallet signature proof required to revoke a vouch

400.description: Malformed signature or already inactive vouch

404.description: Vouch not found or wallet mismatch

notFound.summary: Vouch does not exist or does not belong to wallet

value.message: Vouch not found or wallet mismatch

post.description: Receives Enhanced Transaction events from Helius for on-chain vouch processing.

get.description: Returns the Solana Action metadata for rendering a vouch card in wallets.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: This field is used by the wallet - frontened should write the message in the body

get.description: Creates a time-limited cryptographic challenge used to verify wallet ownership before linking a Solana wallet to the user account. Flow [server gives you a challenge-message >>> wallet signs challenge-message >>> server verifies signature match for challenge-message] This is step 1 in the flow

post.description: Verifies a Solana wallet signature against a previously generated challenge and links the wallet to the authenticated user account.

404.description: Challenge expired or candidate profile missing

post.summary: Verify wallet signature and link wallet

get.description: Employer-only endpoint called before creating escrow on-chain. The backend derives the deterministic escrow id from the job UUID, derives the PDA from the authenticated employer wallet, and returns the exact USDT amount in 6-decimal base units.

parameters.name: jobPostId
parameters.description: Job post id owned by the authenticated employer.

403.description: Authenticated company or wallet does not own the job post.

post.description: Employer-only endpoint called after the employer funds escrow on-chain. The backend verifies the PDA, employer wallet, and funded amount, then records the CREATED -> FUNDED transition. Idempotency: repeating the same confirmation is safe and must not create duplicate state; conflicting funding attempts are rejected.

400.description: Invalid DTO, transaction not found on-chain, wrong amount funded, invalid PDA, or invalid employer wallet.

403.description: Authenticated user is not the employer that owns the job post.

409.description: Escrow is already funded with conflicting details.

post.description: Employer-only endpoint called after the candidate has been set on-chain. The backend verifies the funded escrow and candidate wallet, then records the FUNDED -> CANDIDATE_SET transition. Idempotency: setting the same candidate twice should return the current state; changing an existing candidate is rejected.

400.description: Invalid DTO, escrow not funded yet, invalid wallet format, or on-chain candidate mismatch.

example.message: Escrow must be FUNDED before setting candidate

403.description: Authenticated user is not the employer that owns the job post.

409.description: Candidate already set with different wallet or escrow already resolved.

post.description: Employer-only endpoint called after the on-chain program releases escrow to the candidate. The backend verifies the on-chain resolution and signer ownership, then records CANDIDATE_SET -> RELEASED. Idempotency: repeated release confirmation returns the released state; refunding after release is rejected.

400.description: Invalid DTO, escrow not funded, candidate not set, not resolved on-chain, or wrong signer releasing.

example.message: Candidate must be set before release

403.description: Authenticated user is not the employer that owns the job post.

post.description: Employer-only endpoint called after the on-chain program refunds escrow to the employer. The backend verifies on-chain resolution and ownership, then records FUNDED or CANDIDATE_SET -> REFUNDED. Idempotency: repeated refund confirmation returns the refunded state; release after refund is rejected.

400.description: Invalid DTO, escrow not funded, not resolved on-chain, or wrong signer refunding.

example.message: Escrow must be funded before refund

403.description: Authenticated user is not the employer that owns the job post.

get.description: Employer-only endpoint for polling persisted escrow state and the latest on-chain state. It does not transition state. Only the employer that owns the job post can read it.

parameters.name: jobPostId
parameters.description: Job post id owned by the authenticated employer.

403.description: Authenticated user is not the owner of the job post.

info.title: a16zero Employer API
info.description: Backend MVP for Employer platform features and Account Abstraction auth verification.

totalJobs.description: Total number of jobs created by the company

activeJobs.description: Number of currently active job postings

totalCandidatesShortlisted.description: Total number of shortlisted candidates across all jobs

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

example.summary: Full-stack developer with strong backend capability.

status.description: The decision status for the application

description.example: We are looking for a NestJS expert to build scalable APIs...

example.summary: Full-stack developer with strong backend capability.

description.example: We are looking for a senior engineer to join our team.

jdText.example: We are looking for a Senior Backend Engineer proficient in NestJS and PostgreSQL...

requiredRoleType.description: Primary role type expected for the candidate

parserConfidence.description: Confidence score from the AI parser (0 to 1). Values below ~0.75 should be reviewed manually.

code.description: 6-digit verification code sent to the users email

token.description: Password reset token received via email

voucherWallet.description: Wallet address of the original voucher

signedMessage.description: Signed message proving ownership. Must sign: "revoke-vouch:<vouchId>"

message.description: Vouch message via body (Phantom uses query param instead)
message.example: We built the MVP together in 3 weeks.

account.description: Voucher wallet address (base58). Phantom injects this automatically. For Swagger testing: paste any valid Solana wallet address.

data.description: Vouch message via body. If message query param is present, query param takes priority. Phantom sends message as query param from the href template.

challenge.description: Cryptographic challenge string used to verify wallet ownership

signature.description: Base58 encoded signature of the challenge signed by the wallet private key

message.description: The plaintext message that was signed
message.example:
Link Solana wallet to 16Signals
User: user123
Timestamp: 1713940000000

linked.description: Indicates whether wallet was successfully linked

jobPostId.description: Job post whose on-chain escrow was funded by the employer.
Funded job post that should receive a candidate wallet.

candidateWallet.description: Candidate Solana wallet recorded by the on-chain escrow.

jobPostId.description: Job post whose escrow resolution was observed on-chain.
